title: ⚡ Fix N+1 query in attachment link creation
body:
## 💡 What
Refactored `SyncPayloadAction` to collect attachment link data in an array and use a single bulk `insert()` statement instead of saving each link individually in a loop.

## 🎯 Why
The previous implementation iterated through attachment IDs and executed `$link->save()` for each, creating an N+1 INSERT query anti-pattern. Bulk inserting multiple rows in a single DB query reduces overhead significantly.

## 📊 Measured Improvement
Unable to generate an empirical performance benchmark run locally as the `pgvector` postgres extensions mandated by the testing database schemas fail to run on default `sqlite` testing configuration, and local docker images faced a rate-limiting issue. However, theoretically transforming N `INSERT` queries into 1 bulk `INSERT` query provides an $O(1)$ query complexity versus $O(N)$, mitigating significant application roundtrips for large payloads (like multiple images per form sync).
